stories.headline: New York Times Audits U.S. Banks
stories.summary: John Freed discusses the New York Times' investigative series on U.S. banking stability, utilizing federally available data to assess and report on potentially weak banks.

stories.headline: Mainframes, C.P.U. Time and Your Wallet
stories.summary: This article examines the costs and pricing structures involved in computer-assisted reporting, explaining different fee calculation methods from data processing centers and advising journalists on how to evaluate service options based on their needs and budget.

stories.headline: Tape Drive Trouble? You're Not Alone.
stories.summary: Reporters using Qualstar 1260 tape drives are experiencing frequent malfunctions characterized by drives rocking back and forth, with the Missouri Institute for Computer-Assisted Reporting receiving numerous complaints about the issue. The problems are typically caused by dirty heads or faulty head connectors, particularly involving defective cable connectors that Qualstar switched to approximately a year ago, with replacement cables reportedly solving about half of the reported cases.
